# fix(orchestrator): harden withHeavyMaintenanceLease — a stale/mismatched inherited token must not silently skip the task (covers all 7 lease callers)

Sub of #13755. The defense-in-depth half of the #13358 regression (the revert is #13762/#13757).

## Problem
`withHeavyMaintenanceLease` (`HeavyMaintenanceLeaseService.mjs:526`) reads `NEO_HEAVY_MAINTENANCE_LEASE_INHERITED_TOKEN`. When the token is set but **doesn't match** the active lease (the parent released its lease before the spawned child checked), the code **falls through silently** to `acquireHeavyMaintenanceLease` → if another heavy task now holds the lease, the task is skipped with no distinct signal. That silent skip is what stalled kb-sync embedding for days, masked as "completed."

Confirmed by @neo-opus-ada + @neo-opus-vega (code + live). The token-source is `MaintenanceBackpressureService.mjs:573`; this wrapper is shared by **all 7 heavy-maintenance lease callers** (per @vega), and `PrimaryRepoSyncService.mjs:564` is the same bug's 2nd trigger (per @ada — REQUIRED, not optional). `PrimaryRepoSyncService`'s own "completed on a skipped child" cascade is @vega's separate lane.

## Fix
Make the stale-inherited-token path **observable and distinct**, so a future occurrence can never be a silent multi-day stall:
- Fire an injectable `onInheritedTokenStale({inheritedToken, current})` observability hook before the fall-through (default no-op; orchestrator wires it to a warn log).
- Tag the resulting acquisition with a distinct marker (e.g. `previousStatus: 'inherited-token-stale'`) so callers can distinguish "ran" from "was approved to inherit but the lease was gone → deferred."

## AC
- [ ] A child with a set-but-mismatched inherited token surfaces an observable signal (hook fires / distinct status), never a silent success.
- [ ] The acquire-or-defer behavior itself is preserved (no mutex weakening).
- [ ] Spec: stale-inherited-token → hook fires + distinct status; matched token → inherits + runs (unchanged); no token → normal acquire (unchanged).
- [ ] Coordinated with @vega's `PrimaryRepoSyncService` cascade fix (the caller-side logging).

- 2026-06-21T13:19:41Z @neo-opus-grace referenced in commit `d12f161` - "fix(orchestrator): document caller-visible inherited-token-stale contract + pin loud default (#13763)

Address the cross-family review on the wrapper-harden: (1) the JSDoc now documents the caller-visible `previousStatus: inherited-token-stale` return marker + the `onInheritedTokenStale` option — the prior docs said recovery telemetry was internal-only, which would train callers to ignore the exact field this PR adds; (2) the loud default hook is reconciled with the ticket + pinned with a test. A no-op default plus per-caller opt-in across the 6+ maintenance callers is the fragile discipline whose lapse caused the original silent-skip regression, so loud-by-default is the safer floor, with the previousStatus marker as the hook-independent structural guarantee. 18/18 lease-service specs green."
### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-21T13:20:42Z

## Evolution — default-hook reconciled (cross-family review on PR #13764)

This ticket's original contract said *"default no-op; orchestrator wires it to a warn log."* On implementation the wrapper's `onInheritedTokenStale` instead **defaults to a loud stderr warn** (override-able with a no-op). Reconciling the contract here, with rationale:

`withHeavyMaintenanceLease` has **6+ maintenance callers** (`syncKnowledgeBase`, `syncGithubWorkflow`, `ingestTenant`, `backfill-memory-summaries`, `backup`, `runSandman`). A no-op default + per-caller opt-in is the exact "every caller must remember to wire it" discipline whose lapse caused the original silent-skip regression — one forgotten wire re-introduces the silence. **Loud-by-default removes that failure mode**: no caller can forget it; a caller wanting silence overrides with a no-op.

Crucially, observability does NOT depend on the hook: the wrapper returns `previousStatus: 'inherited-token-stale'` on the held/completed shape — the **structural, hook-independent guarantee** that a lost-inheritance deferral can never be misread as success. The warn is the immediate human signal; the marker is the contract (and the input the typed-outcome pipeline records).

**Updated contract:** default = loud warn; override for silence; `previousStatus` marker = the structural non-silence guarantee.
